questions.scenario: Darkside has 5 seconds remaining. You have 6000 MP.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Edge of Shadow costs 3000 MP and refreshes Darkside. At 5 seconds, refresh it before the 10% damage buff falls off.

questions.scenario: Blood Gauge at 80. Souleater combo ready.
questions.question: What happens with Souleater?
questions.explanation: Souleater gives 20 Blood Gauge. At 80, you'd go to 100 - which is fine, but watch for overcapping.

questions.scenario: Darkside timer at 40 seconds. MP at 9000.
questions.question: Should you Edge of Shadow?
questions.explanation: At 9000 MP, you risk overcapping (max 10000). Use Edge of Shadow to dump MP and extend Darkside.

questions.scenario: You're at 50 Blood. Bloodspiller or save for Delirium?
questions.question: Delirium comes off CD in 20 seconds.
questions.explanation: With 20 seconds until Delirium, you have time to spend and regenerate. Use Bloodspiller to avoid overcapping.

questions.scenario: Combat just started. Darkside isn't active.
questions.question: How do you activate Darkside?
questions.explanation: Darkside activates when you use Edge of Shadow or Flood of Shadow. Use it early to get your damage buff.

questions.scenario: Magic tankbuster incoming. Dark Mind available.
questions.question: Is Dark Mind effective here?
questions.explanation: Dark Mind provides 20% magic damage mitigation. Perfect for magic tankbusters.

questions.scenario: Oblation has 2 charges. Light damage incoming.
questions.question: Should you use Oblation?
questions.explanation: Oblation is only 10% but has 2 charges and short CD. Use it freely for consistent mitigation.

questions.scenario: Huge tankbuster. Shadow Wall and Rampart both available.
questions.question: Which should you use?
questions.explanation: Shadow Wall (30%) + Oblation (10%) handles big hits. Save Rampart for the next attack.

questions.scenario: Physical tankbuster incoming. Only Dark Mind available.
questions.question: Should you use Dark Mind?
questions.explanation: Dark Mind ONLY mitigates magic damage. It does nothing against physical attacks.

questions.scenario: You have Shadow Wall, Oblation, and TBN available.
questions.question: What's the optimal stacking order?
questions.explanation: Shadow Wall (30%) + TBN (25% HP shield) is usually enough. Save Oblation for the next hit.

questions.scenario: You activated Living Dead. You're at 80% HP.
questions.question: What happens if you take fatal damage?
questions.explanation: If you would die during Living Dead, you become Walking Dead instead - unable to die for 10 seconds.

questions.scenario: You're in Walking Dead state. Timer running.
questions.question: What must happen before it ends?
questions.explanation: Walking Dead requires healing to 100% HP before it ends, or you die when the buff expires.

questions.scenario: Walking Dead active. You have access to AoE attacks.
questions.question: What helps you survive?
questions.explanation: During Walking Dead, your attacks heal you. AoE attacks on multiple enemies heal more.

questions.scenario: Living Dead is a 300 second cooldown.
questions.question: How should this affect usage?
questions.explanation: 300s (5 min) means you can use it on planned damage events. Coordinate with healers for optimal use.

questions.scenario: You use Living Dead. Timer expires without taking lethal damage.
questions.question: What happens?
questions.explanation: If you don't take lethal damage during Living Dead, it just expires. No Walking Dead, no penalty.

questions.scenario: Tankbuster coming. TBN available. You have 5000 MP.
questions.question: Should you use TBN?
questions.explanation: Tankbusters will break TBN's shield, giving you Dark Arts (free Edge of Shadow). It's both defensive AND offensive.

questions.scenario: TBN's shield broke completely.
questions.question: What do you get from Dark Arts?
questions.explanation: Dark Arts grants a free Edge of Shadow (or Flood). The 3000 MP you spent on TBN is effectively refunded as damage.

questions.scenario: Light auto-attacks only. TBN available.
questions.question: Should you use TBN?
questions.explanation: If TBN's shield doesn't fully break, you lose the MP and don't get Dark Arts. Save it for bigger damage.

questions.scenario: Party member about to take big damage. Your TBN available.
questions.question: Can you TBN them?
questions.explanation: TBN can be placed on any party member. If THEIR shield breaks, YOU get Dark Arts.

questions.scenario: You have Dark Arts. Edge of Shadow normally costs 3000 MP.
questions.question: How much MP does Dark Arts Edge cost?
questions.explanation: Dark Arts makes your next Edge of Shadow or Flood of Shadow completely free. Pure value.

questions.scenario: Blood Weapon ready. You're about to start your combo.
questions.question: What does Blood Weapon provide?
questions.explanation: Blood Weapon makes your attacks restore MP and Blood Gauge for 15 seconds. Start it before your burst.

questions.scenario: Delirium activated. You have 3 free Bloodspiller charges.
questions.question: What's special about Delirium Bloodspillers?
questions.explanation: Delirium's Bloodspillers cost no Blood Gauge and are guaranteed critical hits. Maximum burst.

questions.scenario: You have 50 Blood. Delirium not available.
questions.question: Should you use Bloodspiller?
questions.explanation: Don't overcap Blood. Use Bloodspiller to spend gauge when Delirium is on CD.

questions.scenario: Blood Weapon has 3 seconds left. Mid-combo.
questions.question: How many GCDs should you fit?
questions.explanation: Blood Weapon lasts 15 seconds. Fit 5 GCDs to maximize MP and Blood generation.

questions.scenario: Blood Weapon active. MP at 9000.
questions.question: Should you Edge of Shadow?
questions.explanation: Blood Weapon generates MP. At 9000, you'll overcap. Dump MP with Edge of Shadow.

questions.scenario: Magic raidwide incoming. Dark Missionary available.
questions.question: What does Dark Missionary provide?
questions.explanation: Dark Missionary gives 10% magic damage mitigation to the entire party for 15 seconds.

questions.scenario: Living Shadow ready. Burst window starting.
questions.question: When should you summon Living Shadow?
questions.explanation: Living Shadow attacks for 24 seconds and does massive damage. Summon during burst to maximize raid buff value.

questions.scenario: Physical raidwide coming. Dark Missionary available.
questions.question: Should you use Dark Missionary?
questions.explanation: Dark Missionary only mitigates magic damage. For physical raidwides, use Reprisal instead.

questions.scenario: You summoned Living Shadow. Boss becomes untargetable.
questions.question: What happens to Living Shadow?
questions.explanation: Living Shadow persists through untargetable phases and continues attacking when the boss returns.

questions.scenario: TBN on party member. Dark Missionary ready. Big hit coming.
questions.question: Can you stack these protections?
questions.explanation: TBN (shield) and Dark Missionary (mitigation) stack. TBN absorbs damage reduced by the mitigation.

questions.scenario: Carve and Spit off cooldown. MP at 6000.
questions.question: What does Carve and Spit provide besides damage?
questions.explanation: Carve and Spit restores MP in addition to dealing damage. Use it to help maintain MP levels.

questions.scenario: Salted Earth ready. Boss standing still.
questions.question: Where should you place Salted Earth?
questions.explanation: Salted Earth is a ground DoT zone. Place it under the boss so it deals damage the entire duration.

questions.scenario: Shadowbringer has 2 charges. Burst window active.
questions.question: How should you use the charges?
questions.explanation: Shadowbringer charges should be used during burst windows to maximize damage under raid buffs.

questions.scenario: Tank swap required. You need to take aggro.
questions.question: What should you do after Provoking?
questions.explanation: After Provoking, you're now main tank. TBN yourself for the incoming damage you'll take.

questions.scenario: Salted Earth is active. Salt and Darkness available.
questions.question: What combo can you do?
questions.explanation: While Salted Earth is active, Salt and Darkness becomes available for additional burst damage.
